---
title-block: false

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: true
    toc-location: left
    anchor-sections: false
    embed-resources: true
    css: styles.css
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    grid: 
      body-width: 1250px
---

```{r}
#
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(here,
               tidyverse,
               tidyr,
               rmarkdown,
               quarto,
               bsicons,
               rlang,
               glue,
               janitor,
               readxl,
               sf,
               knitr,
               kableExtra,
               colorRamps,
               extrafont,
               ggnewscale,
               plotly,
               leaflet)
```


```{r}
#
options(knitr.kable.NA = '--')

here::i_am("Code/sitrep_02.qmd")
```


```{r}
# Date of report generation
datetime_update <- paste0("data as of ", format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y"))
filedate        <- format(Sys.Date(), format = "%Y%m%d")

# Date and start time of common event
date_event     <- lubridate::ymd("2026-02-15")
datetime_event <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "15:00:00"))

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates only)
date_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd(date_event - days(3))
date_epicurve_end   <- Sys.Date() + 2

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates and times)
datetime_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "00:00:00"))
datetime_epicurve_end   <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-20", "00:00:00"))
```


```{r}
# Read in linelist
linelist_raw <- readxl::read_xlsx(paste0(here::here(), "/Data/Linelist_Studley_Park_", filedate, ".xlsx"),
                                  sheet     = "Linelist",
                                  skip      = 2,
                                  guess_max = min(100000, Inf)) %>% 
  #
  janitor::clean_names()

# Data wrangling
linelist_clean <- linelist_raw %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptomatic)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    date_of_birth = janitor::convert_to_date(date_of_birth,
                                             character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    symptom_onset_date = janitor::convert_to_date(symptom_onset_date,
                                                  character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    symptom_end_date = janitor::convert_to_date(symptom_end_date,
                                                character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    symptom_onset_time = hms::as_hms(symptom_onset_time),
    symptom_end_time   = hms::as_hms(symptom_end_time),
    #
    symptom_onset_datetime = lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0(symptom_onset_date, symptom_onset_time)),
    symptom_end_datetime   = lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0(symptom_end_date, symptom_end_time)),
    #
    time_to_onset = symptom_onset_datetime - datetime_event,
    #
    symptoms_status = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(symptom_onset_date) & is.na(symptom_end_date)  ~ "Ongoing",
      !is.na(symptom_onset_date) & !is.na(symptom_end_date) ~ "Resolved",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    #
    symptom_end_date = dplyr::case_when(
      symptoms_status == "Ongoing" ~ lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date()),
      TRUE ~ symptom_end_date),
    #
    age_years = (date_of_birth %--% date_event) / years(1),
    #
    age_group = factor(dplyr::case_when(
      age_years < 10  ~ "0-9 years",
      age_years < 20  ~ "10-19 years",
      age_years < 30  ~ "20-29 years",
      age_years < 40  ~ "30-39 years",
      age_years < 50  ~ "40-49 years",
      age_years < 60  ~ "50-59 years",
      age_years < 70  ~ "60-69 years",
      age_years < 80  ~ "70-79 years",
      age_years < 90  ~ "80-89 years",
      age_years >= 90 ~ "90+ years",
      TRUE ~ "Not stated"),
      levels = c("0-9 years",
                 "10-19 years",
                 "20-29 years",
                 "30-39 years",
                 "40-49 years",
                 "50-59 years",
                 "60-69 years",
                 "70-79 years",
                 "80-89 years",
                 "90+ years",
                 "Not stated")),
    #
    sex = factor(dplyr::case_when(
      sex == "M" ~ "Male",
      sex == "F" ~ "Female",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
      levels = c("Male",
                 "Female")),
    #
    abdo_pain = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(other_specify, "abdo") ~ "Y"),
    #
    household = dplyr::case_when(
      residential_address == "27 Ashe crescent Bellfield, 3081"    ~ "Household 01",
      residential_address == "15 Jordan street Ashwood 3147"       ~ "Household 02",
      residential_address == "13 Maypark ave Ashwood 3147"         ~ "Household 03",
      residential_address == "5 Mclelland Way Burwood East 3151"   ~ "Household 04",
      residential_address == "35 Pembroke Street Surry Hills 3127" ~ "Household 05",
      residential_address == "5 Neale St Preston 3072"             ~ "Household 06",
      residential_address == "1/2 Colvin Ct Glen Waverley 3150"    ~ "Household 07",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
```


```{r}
# Read in survey data
survey_raw <- readxl::read_xlsx(paste0(here::here(), "/Data/Survey_Studley_Park_", filedate, ".xlsx"),
                                guess_max = min(100000, Inf)) %>% 
  #
  janitor::clean_names()

# Exclude test responses
survey_raw <- survey_raw %>% 
  dplyr::filter(id > 5)

# Summary questions about party attendance
survey_attendance <- survey_raw %>% 
  dplyr::select(name = enter_your_full_name,
                attended = did_you_or_someone_in_your_household_attend_a_first_birthday_party_on_sunday_15th_february_2026_at_studley_park_boathouse,
                attended_n = how_many_members_of_your_family_attended_the_event,
                unwell_n = how_many_members_of_your_family_were_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event)

# Extract information for respondents within each survey
survey_clean <- survey_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(name = what_is_the_name_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_survey_for,
                date_of_birth = what_is_the_date_of_birth_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_for,
                symptomatic = has_this_person_been_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event,
                symptom_onset_date = if_yes_what_was_their_symptom_onset_date,
                symptoms = if_yes_what_symptoms_did_they_have,
                symptoms_ongoing = are_they_still_experiencing_gastroenteritis_symptoms,
                symptom_end_date = if_they_have_recovered_when_did_their_symptoms_end,
                party_attend = did_this_person_attend_the_birthday_party_at_studley_park_boathouse_on_15_02_2026,
                party_food = which_of_the_following_foods_did_they_consume_at_the_event,
                faecal_sample = a_stool_poo_sample_helps_us_work_out_the_cause_of_gastroenteritis_have_they_are_they_going_to_provide_a_stool_sample,
                other_notes = is_there_anything_else_you_would_like_to_tell_us) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::bind_rows(survey_raw %>%
                     dplyr::select(name = what_is_the_name_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_survey_for_2,
                                   date_of_birth = what_is_the_date_of_birth_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_for_2,
                                   symptomatic = has_this_person_been_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event_2,
                                   symptom_onset_date = if_yes_what_was_their_symptom_onset_date_2,
                                   symptoms = if_yes_what_symptoms_did_they_have_2,
                                   symptoms_ongoing = are_they_still_experiencing_gastroenteritis_symptoms_2,
                                   symptom_end_date = if_they_have_recovered_when_did_their_symptoms_end_2,
                                   party_attend = did_this_person_attend_the_birthday_party_at_studley_park_boathouse_on_15_02_2026_2,
                                   party_food = which_of_the_following_foods_did_they_consume_at_the_event_2,
                                   faecal_sample = a_stool_poo_sample_helps_us_work_out_the_cause_of_gastroenteritis_have_they_are_they_going_to_provide_a_stool_sample_2,
                                   other_notes = is_there_anything_else_you_would_like_to_tell_us_2)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::bind_rows(survey_raw %>%
                     dplyr::select(name = what_is_the_name_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_survey_for_3,
                                   date_of_birth = what_is_the_date_of_birth_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_for_3,
                                   symptomatic = has_this_person_been_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event_3,
                                   symptom_onset_date = if_yes_what_was_their_symptom_onset_date_3,
                                   symptoms = if_yes_what_symptoms_did_they_have_3,
                                   symptoms_ongoing = are_they_still_experiencing_gastroenteritis_symptoms_3,
                                   symptom_end_date = if_they_have_recovered_when_did_their_symptoms_end_3,
                                   party_attend = did_this_person_attend_the_birthday_party_at_studley_park_boathouse_on_15_02_2026_3,
                                   party_food = which_of_the_following_foods_did_they_consume_at_the_event_3,
                                   faecal_sample = a_stool_poo_sample_helps_us_work_out_the_cause_of_gastroenteritis_have_they_are_they_going_to_provide_a_stool_sample_3,
                                   other_notes = is_there_anything_else_you_would_like_to_tell_us_3)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::bind_rows(survey_raw %>%
                     dplyr::select(name = what_is_the_name_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_survey_for_4,
                                   date_of_birth = what_is_the_date_of_birth_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_for_4,
                                   symptomatic = has_this_person_been_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event_4,
                                   symptom_onset_date = if_yes_what_was_their_symptom_onset_date_4,
                                   symptoms = if_yes_what_symptoms_did_they_have_4,
                                   symptoms_ongoing = are_they_still_experiencing_gastroenteritis_symptoms_4,
                                   symptom_end_date = if_they_have_recovered_when_did_their_symptoms_end_4,
                                   party_attend = did_this_person_attend_the_birthday_party_at_studley_park_boathouse_on_15_02_2026_4,
                                   party_food = which_of_the_following_foods_did_they_consume_at_the_event_4,
                                   faecal_sample = a_stool_poo_sample_helps_us_work_out_the_cause_of_gastroenteritis_have_they_are_they_going_to_provide_a_stool_sample_4,
                                   other_notes = is_there_anything_else_you_would_like_to_tell_us_4)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::bind_rows(survey_raw %>%
                     dplyr::select(name = what_is_the_name_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_survey_for_5,
                                   date_of_birth = what_is_the_date_of_birth_of_the_person_you_are_completing_this_for_5,
                                   symptomatic = has_this_person_been_unwell_with_gastroenteritis_symptoms_in_the_week_after_the_event_5,
                                   symptom_onset_date = if_yes_what_was_their_symptom_onset_date_5,
                                   symptoms = if_yes_what_symptoms_did_they_have_5,
                                   symptoms_ongoing = are_they_still_experiencing_gastroenteritis_symptoms_5,
                                   symptom_end_date = if_they_have_recovered_when_did_their_symptoms_end_5,
                                   party_attend = did_this_person_attend_the_birthday_party_at_studley_park_boathouse_on_15_02_2026_5,
                                   party_food = which_of_the_following_foods_did_they_consume_at_the_event_5,
                                   faecal_sample = a_stool_poo_sample_helps_us_work_out_the_cause_of_gastroenteritis_have_they_are_they_going_to_provide_a_stool_sample_5,
                                   other_notes = is_there_anything_else_you_would_like_to_tell_us_5)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptomatic)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(date_of_birth      = lubridate::ymd(date_of_birth),
                symptom_onset_date = lubridate::ymd(symptom_onset_date),
                symptom_end_date   = lubridate::ymd(symptom_end_date),
                #
                vomiting  = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Vomiting") ~ "Y"),
                diarrhoea = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Diarrhoea") ~ "Y"),
                nausea    = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Nausea") ~ "Y"),
                fever     = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Fever") ~ "Y"),
                abdo_pain = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Abdominal pain") ~ "Y"),
                headache  = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Headache") ~ "Y"),
                lethargy  = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(symptoms, "Lethargy") ~ "Y"))
```


```{r}
# Combined linelist and survey data
linelist_survey <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::select(name,
                date_of_birth,
                symptomatic,
                symptom_onset_date,
                symptom_end_date,
                vomiting,
                diarrhoea,
                nausea,
                fever,
                abdo_pain,
                headache,
                lethargy) %>%
  #
  dplyr::bind_rows(survey_clean %>% 
                     dplyr::select(name,
                                   date_of_birth,
                                   symptomatic,
                                   symptom_onset_date,
                                   symptom_end_date,
                                   vomiting,
                                   diarrhoea,
                                   nausea,
                                   fever,
                                   abdo_pain,
                                   headache,
                                   lethargy)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::distinct(name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  #
  dplyr::mutate(age_years = (date_of_birth %--% date_event) / years(1),
                #
                age_group = factor(dplyr::case_when(
                  age_years < 10  ~ "0-9 years",
                  age_years < 20  ~ "10-19 years",
                  age_years < 30  ~ "20-29 years",
                  age_years < 40  ~ "30-39 years",
                  age_years < 50  ~ "40-49 years",
                  age_years < 60  ~ "50-59 years",
                  age_years < 70  ~ "60-69 years",
                  age_years < 80  ~ "70-79 years",
                  age_years < 90  ~ "80-89 years",
                  age_years >= 90 ~ "90+ years",
                  TRUE ~ "Not stated"),
                  levels = c("0-9 years",
                             "10-19 years",
                             "20-29 years",
                             "30-39 years",
                             "40-49 years",
                             "50-59 years",
                             "60-69 years",
                             "70-79 years",
                             "80-89 years",
                             "90+ years",
                             "Not stated")))
```


```{r}
#| out-width: "100%"
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("Images", "nephu_logo.png"))
```

# Situation Report [#2]{.red-text}

## Outbreak {.unnumbered .unlisted}

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

*Information contained in this Sit Rep is sensitive, confidential, and not intended for wider distribution or use beyond the PH-IMT.*

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

::: {.incident-table}
| Incident name             | Studley Park Boathouse Kew                                        |
|:--------------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------|
| PHESS ID                  | 320266223985                                                      |
| Date/time Sit Rep updated | [`r format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y")`]{.red-text}          |
| Sit Rep approved by (IC)  | [Danielle Martorana]{.red-text}                                   |
| Sit Rep prepared by (IO)  | Alana Little                                                      |

: {tbl-colwidths="[35,65]"}
:::

<div style="height: 1em;"></div>

::: {.callout-note title="Overall summary of incident" icon=false collapse=false}
- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Interviews conducted with [22]{.red-text} attendees across seven households
  - 16/[21]{.red-text} interviewed attendees symptomatic
  - Most common symptoms: [nausea (17/26, 65%), vomiting (17/26, 65%), and lethargy (14/26, 54%)]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- No faecal samples collected [at the time of outbreak notification]{.red-text}
  - [Seven]{.red-text} interviewed attendees across [four]{.red-text} households have agreed to provide faecal samples, relevant Councils arranging sample pot delivery
  - [Six faecal samples from three different households have been sent to MDU]{.red-text}
  - [All six samples are negative for bacterial pathogens and have been forwarded to VIDRL on 26/02/2026]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Menu-based questionnaire [distributed]{.red-text} to birthday party attendees [via party host on 20/02/2026]{.red-text}
  - [Responses received from 13 attendees as of `r format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y")`]{.red-text}
  - [10/13 survey respondents symptomatic]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Boroondara Council notified 18/02/2026 and conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No leftover food available for sampling
  - Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026

:::

<div style="height: 0.125em;"></div>

::: {.callout-tip title="Outbreak case definitions" icon=false collapse=false}
**Confirmed case:** Individuals with a clinically compatible illness who attended the birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026 or who have an epidemiological link to a confirmed case.
:::

***

## Epidemiological Investigations

- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026
  - Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- [26 symptomatic attendees and nine non-symptomatic attendees have been identified through interviews and survey responses]{.red-text}
  - [Interviews conducted with 16 symptomatic attendees and six non-symptomatic attendees]{.red-text}
  - [As of 24/02/2026, survey responses have been received from 10 additional symptomatic attendees and three non-symptomatic attendees]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Two symptomatic survey respondents have also completed an interview
  - Five survey respondents were anonymous, and it is unknown whether these respondents also completed an interview

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- [The most common symptoms reported by symptomatic attendees were nausea (17/26, 65%), vomiting (17/26, 65%), and lethargy (14/26, 54%)]{.red-text}
  - Noted in interviews that young children became unwell first, followed by parents becoming unwell
  - [Median age of symptomatic attendees: 7 years (range 1 to 64 years)]{.red-text}
  - Epicurve by household suggests secondary household transmission occurring

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Birthday party was held in Perch function room
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - Children consumed a minimal amount of food due to their young age
  - Playpen was used by multiple young children at the party

### Epicurves

**Figure 1** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date.

**Figure 2** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time.

**Figure 3** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and household.

**Figure 4** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and age group.

:::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Figure 1

**Number of people reporting symptoms, [interviewed attendees and survey respondents,]{.red-text} by symptom onset date, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_survey %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_date)) %>% 
  #
  count(symptom_onset_date) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(symptom_onset_date, "%d/%m/%Y"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = symptom_onset_date, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 1,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = date_event, xend = date_event, y = 0, yend = max(n, na.rm = TRUE)),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 day",
               date_labels = "%d-%b") +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(date_epicurve_start, date_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.1))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>%
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event date is marked with a dashed purple line.
:::

#### Figure 2

**Number of people reporting symptoms, [interviewed attendees,]{.red-text} by symptom onset date and time, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours")) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))
 
figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 10800,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "3 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date and time",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.25,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- [4x]{.red-text} cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 3

**Number of people reporting symptoms, [interviewed attendees,]{.red-text} by symptom onset date and time and household, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), household) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Household: ", household, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = household, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.55,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center",
                               itemwidth = 10))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- [4x]{.red-text} cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 4

**Number of people reporting symptoms, [interviewed attendees,]{.red-text} by symptom onset date and time and age, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), age_group) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Age: ", age_group, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = age_group, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x    = NULL,
       y    = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.45,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- [4x]{.red-text} cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

::::

***

## Environmental Investigations

- Boroondara Council conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Included deep clean of children’s play area and toilets in the Perch function room

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026
  - Included benches, chopping boards, knives, meat slicer used for platters

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Menu for food served at birthday party and standard menu for Boathouse provided to Council
  - No leftover food available for sampling

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Booking list for day of birthday party and surrounding days provided to Council
  - Further follow-up of booking list not planned at this stage as likely viral gastroenteritis (refer to Risk Assessment section)
  - In addition, booking list consists of >150 individual people who attended on 15/02/2026 so would not be feasible to contact all individuals at this stage
  - Venue does not report illness being reported by other customers

***

## Risk Assessment

- Likely person-person spread of viral gastroenteritis
  - Minimal food consumption by children at the party
  - Children became unwell before parents
  - Likely secondary spread within households (e.g. Household 04)
  - No common food identified by interviewed cases
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified by Council
  - [Six faecal samples collected from three households and all are negative for bacterial pathogens]{.red-text}

***

## Control Measures

- Refer to Environmental Investigations and Risk Assessment sections

***

## Communications

- [Menu-based]{.red-text} questionnaire and advice about gastroenteritis [disseminated]{.red-text} to birthday party attendees via host [on 20/02/2026]{.red-text}

- Council and DH to be informed via usual processes

- No other broad communications planned at this stage

***

## Further Actions Planned

- Await collection of [additional]{.red-text} faecal samples [from interviewed attendees]{.red-text}

- [Await results from collected faecal samples]{.red-text}

- [Await results from environmental sampling]{.red-text}

- [Await further survey responses]{.red-text}

```{r}
#
knitr::knit_exit()
```

## Additional Analyses

### Demographics

```{r}
# Age distribution, interviewed attendees and survey respondents
linelist_survey %>%
  dplyr::group_by(symptomatic) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n      = as.double(n()),
                   min    = round(min(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   max    = round(max(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   mean   = round(mean(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   median = round(median(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., -Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., NaN))) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrrrrr",
               col.names  = c(" ", "n", "Min", "Max", "Mean", "Median")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:5,
                          width = "1.0in") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 2,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 2, "Age (years)" = 4))
```

### Symptoms

```{r}
#
linelist_survey %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(vomiting  = sum(stringr::str_detect(vomiting, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   diarrhoea = sum(stringr::str_detect(diarrhoea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   fever     = sum(stringr::str_detect(fever, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   nausea    = sum(stringr::str_detect(nausea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   abdo_pain = sum(stringr::str_detect(abdo_pain, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   headache  = sum(stringr::str_detect(headache, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   lethargy  = sum(stringr::str_detect(lethargy, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  #
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(),
                      names_to = "symptom") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::rename(n = value) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(symptom = stringr::str_to_sentence(symptom),
                symptom = stringr::str_replace(symptom, "_", " ")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::mutate(percent = round(n / (nrow(linelist_survey %>% dplyr::filter(symptomatic == "Yes"))) * 100, digits = 0)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::arrange(desc(percent)) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrr",
               col.names  = c("Symptom", "n", "%")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:3,
                          width = "1.0in") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 7,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;")
```

### Food History

```{r}
#
survey_clean %>% 
  dplyr::select(symptomatic,
                symptom_onset_date,
                party_food) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(platter_prosciutto = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - prosciutto") ~ "Y"),
                platter_bresaola = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - Bresaola") ~ "Y"),
                platter_cheddar = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - cheddar cheese") ~ "Y"),
                platter_blue = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - blue cheese") ~ "Y"),
                platter_cornichons = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - cornichons") ~ "Y"),
                platter_quince = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - quince paste") ~ "Y"),
                platter_lavosh = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - lavosh crackers") ~ "Y"),
                platter_bark = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Tasting platter - bark crackers") ~ "Y"),
                #
                kids_chicken = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Kids meal - chicken nuggets and chips") ~ "Y"),
                kids_fish = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Kids meal - fish and chips") ~ "Y"),
                #
                pizza_margherita = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Margherita pizza") ~ "Y"),
                pizza_pumpkin = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Pumpkin pizza") ~ "Y"),
                pizza_ham = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Ham and pineapple pizza") ~ "Y"),
                pizza_chicken = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Chicken pizza") ~ "Y"),
                pizza_prosciutto = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Prosciutto pizza") ~ "Y"),
                #
                chips = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Chips with aioli") ~ "Y"),
                #
                fruit_grapes = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - grapes") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_strawberries = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - strawberries") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_watermelon = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - watermelon") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_pineapple = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - pineapple") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_blueberries = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - blueberries") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_raspberries = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - raspberries") ~ "Y"),
                fruit_grapes = dplyr::case_when(stringr::str_detect(party_food, "Fruit platter - grapes") ~ "Y"))
```
